title: santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. ceratophylla
intro: Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. ceratophylla, also known as 'Lion's Tail', is a perennial shrubby plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including aiding digestion, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Santolina rosmarinifolia subsp. ceratophylla can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies.
